segments.speaker: Facilitator
segments.text: Welcome Lisa and Tom. The goal of this session is to understand your team, your goals, and how we can work together effectively.

segments.speaker: Facilitator
segments.text: Let's start with an overview. Tell me about your team.

segments.speaker: Lisa
segments.text: We're the data engineering team. I'm the tech lead and Tom is our senior engineer. We have 6 people total — 4 engineers, a data analyst, and myself.

segments.speaker: Tom
segments.text: Our main responsibility is the data pipeline infrastructure. We ingest data from 15 different sources, transform it, and make it available for analytics and ML teams.

segments.speaker: Facilitator
segments.text: What's your team's primary mission?

segments.speaker: Lisa
segments.text: Reliable, low-latency data delivery. Our SLA is 99.9% uptime and sub-5-minute latency from source to warehouse. We're currently at 99.7% so we have work to do.

segments.speaker: Facilitator
segments.text: What are your top 3 priorities for the next quarter?

segments.speaker: Lisa
segments.text: Number one is hitting our 99.9% SLA. Number two is migrating from Airflow to Dagster for our orchestration layer. Number three is building a self-serve data catalog.

segments.speaker: Tom
segments.text: I'd add that the Dagster migration is the biggest risk. We have 200 Airflow DAGs to migrate and we need to do it without downtime.

segments.speaker: Facilitator
segments.text: What does success look like for your team?

segments.speaker: Lisa
segments.text: Success is when the analytics team can self-serve without filing tickets with us. Right now 40% of our time goes to ad-hoc data requests that should be self-serve.

segments.speaker: Facilitator
segments.text: Let's talk about working preferences. How does your team communicate?

segments.speaker: Tom
segments.text: Slack is primary. We have a team channel and a dedicated alerts channel. For decisions, we use RFCs in Notion. Meetings are minimal — one weekly sync and one biweekly retrospective.

segments.speaker: Lisa
segments.text: We're a remote-first team across 3 time zones. Async communication is really important to us. We don't do ad-hoc video calls unless it's an incident.

segments.speaker: Facilitator
segments.text: Are there tools or processes that are non-negotiable?

segments.speaker: Tom
segments.text: Our CI/CD pipeline is non-negotiable. Every change goes through automated tests and staging before production. No exceptions, even for hotfixes.

segments.speaker: Lisa
segments.text: Also our on-call rotation. Everyone on the team takes a turn, including me. It's important for shared ownership.

segments.speaker: Facilitator
segments.text: What do you need from us to be successful?

segments.speaker: Lisa
segments.text: Clear requirements and realistic timelines. The biggest pain point in the past has been getting requirements that change mid-sprint. We need at least 2 days of lead time for changes.

segments.speaker: Tom
segments.text: And please don't go directly to individual engineers with requests. Route everything through Lisa or our Jira board so we can prioritize properly.

segments.speaker: Facilitator
segments.text: Understood. Anything you'd like us to avoid?

segments.speaker: Lisa
segments.text: Avoid scheduling meetings before 10am Pacific or after 4pm Eastern. Those are our cross-timezone overlap hours and they're reserved for collaboration.

segments.speaker: Facilitator
segments.text: Great. Let me summarize what I've heard and we'll confirm the next steps.

segments.speaker: Facilitator
segments.text: You're a 6-person remote data engineering team focused on reliable data pipelines. Top priorities: hit 99.9% SLA, migrate to Dagster, build data catalog. You prefer async Slack communication, require CI/CD for all changes, and need 2-day lead time on requirements.

segments.speaker: Lisa
segments.text: That's accurate. One thing to add — we'd like a recurring monthly check-in with your team to stay aligned.

segments.speaker: Facilitator
segments.text: We'll set that up. Thanks Lisa and Tom — this was very helpful.
